name: Display a binary mask overlay on an image
description: Tests that the skill guides toward using OverlayData instead of manually blending images or using multiple imshow calls
prompt: I have a grayscale medical image and a binary segmentation mask. Show me how to display the mask on top of the image with some transparency so I can see both.
expected_output: Code using imageshow with OverlayData for the mask, plus OverlayAlpha/OverlayColormap for appearance control. Should not use imshow or manual image blending.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses imageshow for display instead of imshow
Uses the OverlayData property for the mask overlay
Does not call imshow as a function
Sets OverlayAlpha or OverlayColormap for transparency/color control
assertions.expected: OverlayAlpha or OverlayColormap property is set

name: Build an app with an image display panel
description: Tests that the skill guides toward viewer2d parented to uigridlayout with imageshow, not axes with imshow
prompt: Create a simple MATLAB app with a uifigure that has a button to load an image file and a panel to display the loaded image. Use a class-based approach.
expected_output: A classdef app using viewer2d parented to uigridlayout, imageshow initialized with empty data, and .Data property updated on image load. Should not use axes + imshow.

assertions.description: Generated app code passes static analysis via checkcode
Uses viewer2d for the image display container
Uses imageshow to display the image in the viewer
Creates imageshow with empty data on construction, then updates Data property when image is loaded
assertions.expected: imageshow([],... or imageshow('') on init, then .Data = on load

assertions.description: Does not use axes + imshow for the image display

name: Programmatically annotate rectangular regions on an image
description: Tests that the skill guides toward uidraw with Position for programmatic annotations instead of rectangle() or plot()
prompt: I have an image and a list of bounding boxes from an object detector. Each box is [x, y, width, height]. Show me how to draw these boxes on the image with red outlines and labels.
expected_output: Code using imageshow for display and uidraw with Position argument for programmatic rectangle placement. Should not use rectangle() or insertShape.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses imageshow for display
Uses uidraw for annotation placement
Passes Position argument to uidraw for programmatic placement (not interactive)
assertions.expected: Position= or 'Position' argument in uidraw call

assertions.description: Does not call insertObjectAnnotation for drawing boxes

name: Stream updating image data in a loop
description: Tests that the skill guides toward reusing imageshow object and updating .Data with PyramidSmoothing set to nearest for streaming performance
prompt: I need to display a live feed of images updating in a loop. Each iteration produces a new frame. How do I set this up for smooth, fast display?
expected_output: Code using viewer2d and imageshow with PyramidSmoothing='nearest', initialized with empty data, then updating .Data in a loop with drawnow. Should not recreate imageshow each iteration.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses imageshow for display
Sets PyramidSmoothing to nearest for streaming performance
Updates .Data property in the loop instead of recreating imageshow
assertions.expected: .Data = inside loop body, imageshow created once before loop

assertions.description: Calls drawnow to flush display updates

name: Generate an animation by capturing frames
description: Tests that the skill guides toward using getframe with viewer (not figure/axes) so the viewer waits for updates before capturing
prompt: I want to generate an animated GIF from a sequence of processed images. How do I capture each frame for the animation?
expected_output: Code using viewer2d and imageshow to display frames, with getframe(viewer) to capture each frame. Should pass the viewer to getframe, not a figure or axes handle.

assertions.description: Generated code passes static analysis (checkcode) without errors. Note: getframe loops time out in MCP execution, so this eval uses static analysis only.
assertions.expected: Code passes checkcode with zero errors

assertions.description: Uses imageshow for display
Uses getframe to capture frames
Passes the viewer object to getframe so it waits for rendering to complete
assertions.expected: getframe(viewer) where viewer is the viewer2d object

assertions.description: Uses viewer2d as the display container

name: Interactive painting for multi-class labeling
description: Tests that generated code correctly creates an Image object and would pass it to uipaint. Validates via partial execution: runs the setup code (imageshow creation) and verifies the object type is valid for uipaint. imageshow CANNOT be parented directly to uifigure — it requires a viewer2d parent or no parent.
prompt: I have a satellite image and want to interactively paint regions to label them as 'water', 'vegetation', and 'urban'. After I finish painting each class, automatically compute the area percentage of that label. Show the labeled regions as a colored overlay on the image.
expected_output: Code that calls imageshow (either without Parent or with Parent=viewer2d), stores the Image object, and passes it to uipaint. Must not parent imageshow directly to uifigure/uiaxes (runtime error).

assertions.description: Partial execution: run ONLY the imageshow setup line(s) from the generated code. imageshow parented directly to uifigure will hard error: 'Create a valid Viewer parent with the viewer2d or viewer3d function'. The setup must succeed to produce a valid Image object for uipaint.

The variable passed as first argument to uipaint is the Image object returned by imageshow (or its parent viewer). Verify by checking that the same variable name appears as both the imageshow output and the uipaint first argument.
assertions.expected: Same variable used: obj = imageshow(...); uipaint(obj, ...) — NOT uipaint(ax, ...) or uipaint(fig, ...)

assertions.description: Uses OverlayValue name-value argument in uipaint calls to differentiate classes
assertions.expected: OverlayValue appears in uipaint calls with different values per class

assertions.description: Sets .OverlayData on the Image object to display the label overlay
assertions.expected: obj.OverlayData = ... where obj is the imageshow output variable

assertions.description: Does not use uiaxes anywhere (imageshow cannot be parented to uiaxes)

name: Side-by-side linked viewers for image comparison
description: Tests that the skill guides toward viewer2d parented to a layout container (uigridlayout or uipanel), imageshow with Parent=viewer, and linkviewers with an array of viewer objects
prompt: Display a pre-operative and post-operative medical image side-by-side in two viewer panels. Link their pan and zoom so when I zoom into a region in one, the other follows.
expected_output: Code creating a uifigure with uigridlayout, two viewer2d objects parented to layout containers, imageshow with Parent= each viewer, and linkviewers([v1,v2]). Should not use axes, subplot, or figure with multiple axes.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB (linkviewers does not block)
Parents viewer2d to a layout container (uigridlayout, uipanel, or uifigure), not to a plain figure() call
assertions.expected: viewer2d(gridLayout) or viewer2d(panel) or viewer2d(fig) where fig is uifigure, NOT viewer2d(figure())

assertions.description: Passes the viewer2d object as Parent to imageshow (not axes or panel)
assertions.expected: imageshow(im, Parent=v) where v is a viewer2d object

assertions.description: linkviewers receives a vector of viewer2d variables, not figure handles or axes
assertions.expected: linkviewers([v1, v2]) where v1 and v2 are the same viewer2d variables used earlier

assertions.description: Does not use axes, subplot, or linkaxes

name: Draw multiple annotations in one continuous session
description: Tests correct uidraw setup: imageshow must be created with a valid parent (viewer2d or no parent, NOT uifigure directly), and uidraw must receive an Image or Viewer object. Validates via partial execution of setup code. uidraw with Wait='multiple' is interactive so only setup is executed.
prompt: I have a microscopy image with dozens of cells. Let me draw circle annotations around all cells in one continuous session without having to re-invoke the drawing tool each time. When I'm done, extract the positions and radii of all circles and export them to a table.
expected_output: Code that creates imageshow correctly (without parent or with viewer2d parent), passes the Image/Viewer to uidraw with Wait='multiple', and extracts data from the ROI array. Must not parent imageshow to uifigure directly (hard error).

assertions.description: Partial execution: run the imageshow setup line(s). If code uses imageshow(im, Parent=uifigure_handle) it will hard error: 'Create a valid Viewer parent with the viewer2d or viewer3d function'. Must use imageshow(im) or imageshow(im, Parent=viewer2d_obj).
The first argument to uidraw is the Image object (imageshow output) or a viewer2d object. uidraw errors if given a figure: 'Specify parent as a valid Viewer object'.
assertions.expected: uidraw(imgObj, ...) or uidraw(viewer, ...) where imgObj/viewer is from imageshow or viewer2d

assertions.description: Uses Wait='multiple' in the uidraw call for continuous batch drawing
assertions.expected: Wait="multiple" appears in the uidraw call

assertions.description: Does not use drawcircle, drawfreehand, or other legacy interactive ROI functions
uidraw is called exactly once (not in a loop). Batch drawing is handled by Wait='multiple', not by repeated uidraw calls.
assertions.expected: Single uidraw call with Wait='multiple'. No loop around uidraw.

name: Non-uniform overlay transparency with OverlayAlphamap
description: Tests correct OverlayAlphamap usage: set on the Image object (output of imageshow) alongside OverlayData, using a ramp mode or vector, and not falling back to scalar OverlayAlpha or manual blending
prompt: I have a depth map and an RGB image from the same scene. Display the RGB image with an overlay where transparency varies based on the overlay data values — specifically, I want higher overlay values to be more opaque and lower values to fade out. Do not manually blend pixels.
expected_output: Code storing imageshow output in a variable, setting both OverlayData and OverlayAlphamap as properties on that object (or as name-value pairs). OverlayAlphamap should be 'linear', 'quadratic', 'cubic', or a custom vector. Should not use AlphaData on axes, set scalar OverlayAlpha, or manually compute blended RGB.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Stores the imageshow output in a variable and sets OverlayData and OverlayAlphamap on that same object (or passes both as name-value pairs to imageshow)
assertions.expected: Pattern: obj = imageshow(im, OverlayData=data); obj.OverlayAlphamap = ... OR imageshow(im, OverlayData=data, OverlayAlphamap=...)

assertions.description: OverlayAlphamap is set to 'linear', 'quadratic', 'cubic', or a column vector — NOT a scalar value and NOT confused with OverlayAlpha
assertions.expected: OverlayAlphamap = "linear" or "quadratic" or "cubic" or linspace(...)' or custom vector

assertions.description: Does not use AlphaData on axes or set() with axes handle for transparency
Does not manually compute blended RGB (no arithmetic combining image and overlay pixel values)

name: Programmatically zoom into a specific image region
description: Tests that the skill guides toward CameraViewport for programmatic zoom instead of xlim/ylim which error on viewer2d. The viewer is not an axes — axis limit functions are invalid.
prompt: I have a large image displayed with imageshow and I detected an object at bounding box [250, 100, 150, 120]. Programmatically zoom the display to focus on just that region so the user can inspect it closely.
expected_output: Code using viewer2d with imageshow, then setting CameraViewport to the bounding box to zoom the display. Should not use xlim, ylim, axis, or zoom on the viewer.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets CameraViewport on the viewer to zoom to the region of interest
Uses imageshow for display
Does not call xlim to set horizontal bounds (errors on viewer2d)
Does not call ylim to set vertical bounds (errors on viewer2d)
